feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: New Suspected Hantavirus Case Reported In Spain (Live Updates)
entry.summary: The MV Hondius is en route to the Canary Islands, where it will be allowed to dock for the first time since a hantavirus outbreak was confirmed on board.

entry.title: Babies are bleeding to death as parents reject a vitamin shot given at birth
entry.summary: Vitamin K shots, which help the blood to clot, are one of three key interventions for newborns, along with an antibiotic eye ointment and the hepatitis B vaccine.

entry.title: Hantavirus latest updates: U.S. among countries searching for passengers who left Dutch cruise ship after 1st death
entry.summary: Three people — a Dutch couple and a German national — have died in the outbreak on the MV Hondius. There are eight confirmed or suspected cases in total.

entry.title: 2 Hantavirus Cruise Passengers Are Texas Residents (Live Updates)
entry.summary: The MV Hondius is en route to the Canary Islands, where it will be allowed to dock for the first time since a hantavirus outbreak was confirmed on board.

entry.title: Eating eggs could cut Alzheimer’s risk by 27%
entry.summary: Eating eggs might do more than just start your day—it could help protect your brain. Researchers found that people 65 and older who eat eggs regularly have a significantly lower risk of developing Alzheimer’s disease, with daily or near-daily consumption link…

entry.title: As measles roars back, scientists find antibodies that could offer protection
entry.summary: Scientists just took a step toward developing a therapy that could prevent someone from getting measles after they have been exposed.

entry.title: Health officials track dozens who left hantavirus-stricken ship after 1st fatality
entry.summary: Oceanwide Expeditions has revealed that 30 passengers disembarked from its cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak on April 24, on the remote island of St. Helena, nearly two weeks after the first passenger died.

entry.title: Woman, 48, Dies After Grueling Day of Plastic Surgery Procedures: Lawsuit
entry.summary: “She saw herself, I think, coming out of the surgery with the body she wanted,” her widower said.

entry.title: Why sitting all day is so bad for you — and what to do about it
entry.summary: Sitting isn’t exactly the “new smoking,” but it still isn’t a healthy habit. Here’s what too much sedentary time is doing to you and how to move more.

entry.title: Hantavirus latest updates: Confirmed cases rise to 5 from Dutch cruise ship, WHO says
entry.summary: Three people – a Dutch couple and a German national – have died in the outbreak on the MV Hondius. There are eight confirmed or suspected cases in total.

entry.title: Babies are bleeding to death as parents reject vitamin shot given at birth
entry.summary: More and more newborns are dying from an easily preventable condition.

entry.title: An army of ticks is sending more Marylanders to the ER
entry.summary: Across the country, emergency department visits for tick bites are up more than 25% from last year, according to federal data. Maryland and other northeastern states are looking like ground zero.

entry.title: Scientists find a way to stop dangerous belly fat as we age
entry.summary: Aging doesn’t just add fat—it redistributes it in risky ways, pushing more into the abdomen where it can harm health. Scientists found that testosterone plays a key role in this shift. In older women recovering from hip fractures, a testosterone gel combined …

entry.title: Your heart rate is more uneven than you think. This is what it says about your health
entry.summary: Micro-fluctuations in the time between heartbeats are proving a helpful indicator of mental health, stress levels, exercise capacity and even how well you are ageing.

entry.title: KLM flight attendant hospitalized after contact with hantavirus cruise ship passenger
entry.summary: A KLM flight attendant from Haarlem has been hospitalized due to a possible hantavirus infection. She came into contact with the 69-year-old Dutch woman who died of the virus in Johannesburg, South Africa. The flight attendant is in isolation at Amsterdam UMC…

entry.title: Scientists Reveal How Exercise Benefits Every Part of Your Body
entry.summary: It is tempting to understand muscle's role in the body as a simple mechanical motor.

entry.title: Georgia monitoring two residents who traveled on cruise ship with hantavirus outbreak
entry.summary: The two residents show no signs of infection, according to the Georgia DPH.

entry.title: New Jersey beaver attack: 8-year-old boy injured by animal at park in Mahwah, NJ
entry.summary: Police said the child was fishing from the shoreline when a beaver came out of the water and charged at him.

entry.title: Marty Makary Has All the Right Enemies
entry.summary: The FDA is rebuilding trust, not suppressing ‘science.’

entry.title: East Valley measles outbreak grows: Where you may have been exposed
entry.summary: The county's case count has doubled in the last month, and the exposure sites are even more numerous.

entry.title: Stop Popping Pills Without Thinking
entry.summary: Spring has long sprung, and our allergies are in full swing. Luckily, modern medicine has plenty of solutions—but think twice before you grab that pill bottle off the pharmacy shelf. Full Story

entry.title: A New Study Found Brain-Eating Amoeba at 3 Popular National Park Sites
entry.summary: A new study confirms high concentrations of Naegleria fowleri at popular soaking spots across Yellowstone and Grand Teton National Parks. As water temperatures rise, experts warn so do the risk for infection.

entry.title: Drinking Coffee May Have a Surprising Benefit, New Study Says
entry.summary: Coffee may support the gut-brain axis by influencing the microbiome and immune health, according to new research published in 'Nature Communications'.

entry.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship leaves Cape Verde after three evacuated
entry.summary: A British man is among three evacuees sent to the Netherlands after displaying symptoms while aboard the MV Hondius.

entry.title: HIV-1 signalling remodels nuclear pores to licence infection
entry.summary: HIV-1 capsid nuclear import at the nuclear pore complex is a bottleneck to resting T cell infection, but HIV-1 overcomes this by triggering receptor-mediated signalling during cell–cell spread to drive nuclear import and licence infection.

entry.title: RNA-triggered cell killing with CRISPR–Cas12a2
entry.summary: Cas12a2 enables RNA-triggered, sequence-specific killing of eukaryotic cells via widespread DNA shredding, allowing selective elimination of cells on the basis of gene expression, including virus-infected or mutation-bearing cells.

entry.title: 'This is a big outbreak': Nearly 1 in 5 affected by TB at SF school
entry.summary: New testing is starting at Archbishop Riordan High School as officials work to keep tuberculosis cases from spreading again.

entry.title: Moderna’s mRNA flu vaccine more effective than standard shot in late-stage trial
entry.summary: Moderna’s mRNA flu vaccine gave more protection against illness than the standard flu shot in a phase 3 clinical trial, according to published results.

entry.title: Beaver that bit ‘several people’ in New Jersey tests positive for rabies
entry.summary: 'On Sunday, May 3rd, it was reported that a beaver at Lake Henry had contact with several people and has since tested positive for rabies,' the Mahwah Health Department said.

entry.title: Scientists Discover How Coffee Impacts Memory, Mood, and Gut Health
entry.summary: Coffee alters gut bacteria and improves mood and cognition, with both caffeinated and decaf offering distinct mental health benefits.

entry.title: Even the unconscious brain can learn — and predict what you’ll say next
entry.summary: Neuronal recordings of people under anaesthesia show that their brains are processing words and sounds.

entry.title: Non-invasive profiling of the tumour microenvironment with spatial ecotypes
entry.summary: Multimodal machine learning reveals that tumour microenvironments can be decomposed into spatially organized multicellular ecosystems, termed spatial ecotypes, that can be accessed non-invasively via liquid biopsy and used to profile individual cancers a…

entry.title: Man Gets Horrific Case of Diarrhea After Overdosing on Experimental GLP-1
entry.summary: The man was going to the bathroom up to 30 times a day.

entry.title: 3 patients are being evacuated to Europe from cruise ship with hantavirus outbreak
entry.summary: The U.N. health agency says three patients on a cruise ship with suspected hantavirus infections are being flown to the Netherlands. The ship involved in a deadly outbreak remains off Cape Verde. The World Health Organization says eight cases have been record…

entry.title: FDA vaccine studies censored by Trump admin after finding benefits of shots
entry.summary: FDA has suppressed studies on COVID-19 vaccines and Shingrix, a shingles vaccine.

entry.title: Large study finds a strong link between depression and problem cannabis use
entry.summary: A new meta-analysis of 55 studies involving more than 3 million people has found that 31% of individuals with cannabis use disorder (CUD) also struggle with major depressive disorder (MDD). While a link between these two conditions has been known for some tim…

entry.title: NYC measles case leads health officials to notify Manhattan restaurants, other venues
entry.summary: The outreach follows a positive case that officials have linked to foreign travel.

entry.title: While patients lay unconscious under anesthesia, their brains kept decoding stories and preparing for what came next
entry.summary: Baylor College of Medicine researchers have found that the human brain is capable of sophisticated language processing while in an unconscious state from general anesthesia. The findings, published in Nature, challenge what we know about the role of conscious…

entry.title: Male sex hormone loss aids brain tumour growth
entry.summary: Depletion of sex hormones that enable male development induces neuroinflammation that disrupts hormonal signalling and immune responses against brain tumours.

entry.title: A new kind of CRISPR could treat viral infection and cancer by shredding sick cells' DNA
entry.summary: A new kind of CRISPR that destroys cells rather than gene editing them has shown potential for killing sick cells while leaving healthy cells untouched. The technology has largely been tested in cells in a dish, but if it can be applied to organisms, it could…

entry.title: What Happens to Your Blood Pressure When You Eat Blueberries Regularly
entry.summary: Blueberries can help lower blood pressure, making them a great addition to your diet if you've been diagnosed with hypertension.

entry.title: Movement Triggers a Hidden 'Brain Cleaning' Mechanism, Study Shows
entry.summary: We already know that moving your body is important for brain health, but a new study reveals a possible reason why: It could be triggering a kind of hydraulic pump that flushes out fluid in the brain.

entry.title: Tick bite ER visits surge across Northeast, CDC data show
entry.summary: Upstate Medical University’s Tick Testing Laboratory in Syracuse is predicting a heavy tick season in the Hudson Valley, Capital Region and central New York

entry.title: Diagnostic dilemma: A woman turned black and blue weeks after starting a new medication
entry.summary: A woman's sudden hyperpigmentation was a side effect of a medicine she was taking.

entry.title: Babies Are Bleeding to Death as Parents Reject a Vitamin Shot Given at Birth
entry.summary: A lifesaving injection given at birth to prevent severe bleeding has become collateral damage of the anti-vaccine movement.

entry.title: Colon cancer breakthrough keeps patients cancer-free for nearly 3 years
entry.summary: A short burst of immunotherapy before surgery is delivering surprisingly powerful results for a specific type of colorectal cancer. Patients in a UK-led trial who received just nine weeks of pembrolizumab prior to surgery have remained cancer-free nearly thre…

entry.title: Eggs and brain health: New study cracks open Alzheimer’s link
entry.summary: A long-term study suggests that eating eggs regularly may be associated with a lower risk of developing Alzheimer’s disease in older adults.

entry.title: Common knee surgery found ineffective, may make things worse
entry.summary: A major 10-year clinical trial is turning one of the world’s most common knee surgeries on its head. Researchers found that trimming a damaged meniscus—a procedure long believed to relieve pain—offers no real benefit over placebo surgery. Even more surprising…

entry.title: Spain agrees to let hantavirus-hit cruise ship dock in Canary Islands
entry.summary: Three people have died since the outbreak in April, while two crew members on board the ship require urgent care.

entry.title: HT Health Talk: Is there minimum and maximum age for Ozempic? Bengaluru doctor answers 10 FAQs about weight loss drugs | Health
entry.summary: HT Health Talk: GLP-1 drugs are revolutionary, but require nutritional monitoring and strength training to remain safe and effective, per a doctor. | Health
